title: najas flexilis var. guadalupensis
intro: Najas flexilis var. guadalupensis, also known as the Mexican water-willow or Guadalupe water-willow, is a submersed aquatic plant that belongs to the Najadaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Najas flexilis var. guadalupensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and act as an expectorant to help relieve congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the roots to make a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
